title: [Cleanup] Extract shared constants (skill IDs, colors, validation patterns)
description:
Done — created lib/constants.ts and backend/src/constants.ts with VALID_SKILL_IDS, RECIPE_ID_REGEX, SKILL_COLOR_NAME. Replaced 6 inline skill ID arrays and 5 regex patterns across backend routes. Updated frontend validation.ts to use centralized constants.

### What's wrong
Skill IDs, color maps, and validation regex patterns are hardcoded in 10+ locations across frontend and backend. Any change requires updating multiple files.

### What to fix

#### 1. Frontend constants — create lib/constants.ts
```typescript
export const VALID_SKILL_IDS = ['basic-cooking', 'heat-control', 'flavor-building', 'air-fryer', 'indian-cuisine'] as const;
export type SkillId = typeof VALID_SKILL_IDS[number];

export const SKILL_COLOR_MAP: Record<SkillId, { tailwind: string; bg: string; text: string; border: string }> = {
  'basic-cooking': { tailwind: 'blue', bg: 'bg-blue-500', text: 'text-blue-600', border: 'border-blue-200' },
  'heat-control': { tailwind: 'orange', bg: 'bg-orange-500', text: 'text-orange-600', border: 'border-orange-200' },
  'flavor-building': { tailwind: 'purple', bg: 'bg-purple-500', text: 'text-purple-600', border: 'border-purple-200' },
  'air-fryer': { tailwind: 'green', bg: 'bg-green-500', text: 'text-green-600', border: 'border-green-200' },
  'indian-cuisine': { tailwind: 'amber', bg: 'bg-amber-500', text: 'text-amber-600', border: 'border-amber-200' },
};

export const COOKBOOK_COLORS = {
  bg: 'bg-amber-50',
  border: 'border-amber-200',
  accent: 'bg-amber-500',
  text: 'text-stone-700',
};

export const RECIPE_ID_REGEX = /^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]{2,49}$/;
```

#### 2. Backend constants — create backend/.../src/constants.ts
```typescript
export const VALID_SKILL_IDS = ['basic-cooking', 'heat-control', 'flavor-building', 'air-fryer', 'indian-cuisine'];
export const RECIPE_ID_REGEX = /^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]{2,49}$/;
```

#### 3. Replace all hardcoded instances
- Frontend: learning-path.tsx, skill-card.tsx, skill/[skillId]/page.tsx, photo-node.tsx — replace inline color maps
- Backend: routes/progression.ts, routes/progress.ts, routes/recipes.ts, routes/skills.ts — replace skill ID arrays
- Backend: routes/recipes.ts, routes/favorites.ts — replace duplicated recipeIdValidation regex

### Subtasks
1. Create lib/constants.ts with skill IDs, color map, cookbook colors, regex
2. Create backend/.../src/constants.ts with skill IDs, regex
3. Replace all hardcoded skill ID arrays in backend routes (5+ files)
4. Replace all color maps in frontend components (3+ files)
5. Replace duplicated recipeIdValidation in backend routes
6. Verify build passes

### Depends on
task_195

### Acceptance criteria
- Given skill IDs, when grepped as inline arrays, then only found in constants files
- Given color values, when grepped, then sourced from SKILL_COLOR_MAP
- Given build, when run, then passes with no errors

### Files to create/modify
- lib/constants.ts (new)
- backend/node-services/api-server/src/constants.ts (new)
- components/learning-path.tsx (import from constants)
- components/skill-card.tsx (import from constants)
- app/skill/[skillId]/page.tsx (import from constants)
- backend/node-services/api-server/src/routes/progression.ts
- backend/node-services/api-server/src/routes/progress.ts
- backend/node-services/api-server/src/routes/recipes.ts
- backend/node-services/api-server/src/routes/skills.ts
- backend/node-services/api-server/src/routes/favorites.ts

title: [Cleanup] Fix TypeScript any types across frontend
description:
## LOW-MEDIUM — Replace all 'any' type escapes with proper TypeScript types

### What's wrong
Multiple `any` types across frontend bypass TypeScript's type safety, hiding potential bugs and making refactoring harder.

### What to fix

#### 1. hooks/use-recipes.ts
- Line ~11: `mapRecipe(raw: any)` → Create `RawRecipeResponse` interface matching actual API shape
- Line ~23: `(si: any)` → Type as `RawStructuredIngredient`
- Line ~45: `Record<string, any>` → Type filter params properly
- Line ~145: `as any` cast on tip → Type the tip response

#### 2. hooks/use-social.ts
- Lines ~240, ~296: `catch (err: any)` → Create ApiError type guard:
```typescript
function isApiError(err: unknown): err is { status: number; message: string } {
  return typeof err === 'object' && err !== null && 'status' in err;
}
```

#### 3. hooks/use-pwa-install.ts
- Line ~26: `(navigator as any).standalone` → Extend Navigator interface:
```typescript
declare global {
  interface Navigator { standalone?: boolean; }
}
```

#### 4. lib/api/favorites.ts
- `FavoritesListResponse.recipes: any[]` → `Recipe[]`

#### 5. lib/types.ts vs lib/api/auth.ts
- Consolidate duplicate `UserProfile` interfaces into single source of truth in lib/types.ts

#### 6. app/skill/[skillId]/page.tsx
- Type `SKILL_META` with proper interface instead of implicit object type

#### 7. components/learning-path.tsx
- Export `CookbookNode` type instead of defining inline

### Subtasks
1. Create RawRecipeResponse, RawStructuredIngredient interfaces
2. Refactor mapRecipe() to use proper types
3. Create isApiError() type guard in lib/api/client.ts
4. Fix catch blocks in use-social.ts
5. Extend Navigator interface for PWA
6. Fix FavoritesListResponse to use Recipe[]
7. Consolidate UserProfile interfaces
8. Type SKILL_META and export CookbookNode
9. Verify build passes with strict mode

### Depends on
task_199

### Acceptance criteria
- Given codebase, when grepped for ': any', then count reduced by 80%+
- Given TypeScript strict mode, when built, then no type errors
- Given refactored types, when API responses checked, then types match actual data

### Files to create/modify
- hooks/use-recipes.ts
- hooks/use-social.ts
- hooks/use-pwa-install.ts
- lib/api/favorites.ts
- lib/types.ts
- lib/api/auth.ts
- app/skill/[skillId]/page.tsx
- components/learning-path.tsx

title: [Cleanup] Split learning-path.tsx & remove stale UI code
description:
## LOW — Split oversized component and clean up stale UI patterns

### What's wrong
learning-path.tsx is 667 LOC — the largest non-shadcn component. It contains 3 distinct concerns mixed together: path nodes, chef character, and cookbook mode. Also, photo-node.tsx has an unused isCompleted prop.

### What to fix

#### 1. Split learning-path.tsx into focused components

**components/path-node.tsx** (~140 lines)
- Extract the PathNode sub-component (current lines ~112-255)
- Props: recipe, index, isCompleted, onToggle, colorMap, mode

**components/chef-character.tsx** (~20 lines)
- Extract chef emoji section (current lines ~257-277)
- Simple presentational component

**components/cookbook-node-list.tsx** (~100 lines)
- Extract cookbook mode rendering logic
- Props: recipes, photos, onUpload, colorMap

**components/learning-path.tsx** (~400 lines after extraction)
- Orchestrator that imports and composes the above
- Handles the winding path layout, SVG lines, milestone badges

#### 2. Remove unused props
- photo-node.tsx: Remove `isCompleted` from props interface if confirmed unused in rendering

#### 3. Clean up chart.tsx selector
- Break the 400+ character Tailwind selector string into multiple lines or a constant array

### Subtasks
1. Create components/path-node.tsx with extracted PathNode
2. Create components/chef-character.tsx
3. Create components/cookbook-node-list.tsx
4. Refactor learning-path.tsx to import new components
5. Remove unused isCompleted prop from photo-node.tsx
6. Clean up chart.tsx Tailwind selector
7. Verify build and visual regression (manual check)

### Depends on
task_201

### Acceptance criteria
- Given learning-path.tsx, when measured, then under 450 LOC
- Given new components, when imported, then render identically to before
- Given build, when run, then passes

### Files to create/modify
- components/path-node.tsx (new)
- components/chef-character.tsx (new)
- components/cookbook-node-list.tsx (new)
- components/learning-path.tsx (refactor)
- components/photo-node.tsx (remove unused prop)
- components/ui/chart.tsx (clean selector)

title: [Cleanup] Complete deprecated token migration & centralize localStorage
description:
Done — created lib/storage.ts (centralized localStorage access with KEYS constant, CACHE_TIMES docs). Removed getToken from upload-helpers.ts (relies on httpOnly cookies via credentials: 'include'). Removed getToken/setToken from barrel exports. Fixed RawRecipeResponse type mapping in use-recipes.ts. Build passes.

## LOW — Finish httpOnly cookie migration and centralize storage access

### What's wrong
1. getToken()/setToken() are marked @deprecated but still actively used in 5+ files. The migration from localStorage tokens to httpOnly cookies is incomplete.
2. localStorage is accessed directly with hardcoded keys in multiple places without a central abstraction.
3. Query cache times (staleTime/gcTime) are inconsistent across hooks with no documented strategy.

### What to fix

#### 1. Complete token migration
Audit all usages of getToken()/setToken():
- lib/api/client.ts (lines 168, 208, 237) — internal apiClient usage
- lib/api/social.ts (line 73) — avatar upload
- lib/api/uploads.ts (line 8) — photo upload
- hooks/use-recipes.ts (line 8) — photo upload
- hooks/use-progression.ts (line 7) — progression API calls

For each: determine if httpOnly cookie is already sent (credentials: 'include'), and if so, remove the Authorization header fallback. If not, add credentials: 'include'.

Once all callers migrated, remove getToken()/setToken() exports.

#### 2. Centralize localStorage — create lib/storage.ts
```typescript
const KEYS = {
  VIEW_MODE: 'cookquest-view-mode',
  ONBOARDING: 'cookquest-onboarding-seen',
  // ... other keys
} as const;

export const storage = {
  getViewMode: () => localStorage.getItem(KEYS.VIEW_MODE),
  setViewMode: (mode: string) => localStorage.setItem(KEYS.VIEW_MODE, mode),
  // ... etc
};
```

#### 3. Document and standardize cache times
Add a comment block in a shared location explaining cache strategy:
- User-specific data (progress, profile): staleTime 1min, gcTime 5min
- Global data (recipes, skills): staleTime 5min, gcTime 10min  
- Feed data: staleTime 30sec, gcTime 2min

### Subtasks
1. Audit all getToken()/setToken() call sites
2. Test if httpOnly cookies work for each endpoint
3. Remove localStorage token usage where cookies work
4. Create lib/storage.ts for remaining localStorage keys
5. Replace direct localStorage calls with storage abstraction
6. Standardize cache times in hooks with comments
7. Verify build and auth flow still works

### Depends on
task_202

### Acceptance criteria
- Given getToken/setToken, when grepped, then zero imports outside client.ts (or fully removed)
- Given localStorage.getItem, when grepped, then only found in lib/storage.ts
- Given cache times, when inspected, then follow documented strategy
- Given auth flow, when tested, then login/logout still works

### Files to create/modify
- lib/storage.ts (new)
- lib/api/client.ts (remove deprecated exports)
- lib/api/social.ts (remove getToken import)
- lib/api/uploads.ts (remove getToken import)
- hooks/use-recipes.ts (remove getToken import)
- hooks/use-progression.ts (remove getToken import)
- hooks/use-social.ts (standardize cache times)
- lib/stores/recipe-store.ts (use storage abstraction)

title: [Backend] Migration 024 + Classical CV photo analysis service (6 image signals)
description:
## HIGH — Non-AI photo verification: database schema + CV analysis engine

### What's wrong
Currently any image passing basic MIME/magic-byte validation gets +80 XP and unlocks recipes. Users can upload screenshots, stock photos, or random images to game progression.

### What to build

#### 1. Migration `024_photo_verification` in `database.ts`

```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS photo_verifications (
  id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  photo_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
  user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
  -- CV signals (0.0 - 1.0)
  color_diversity REAL DEFAULT 0,
  saturation_warmth REAL DEFAULT 0,
  texture_variance REAL DEFAULT 0,
  multi_region REAL DEFAULT 0,
  specular_highlights REAL DEFAULT 0,
  circular_contours REAL DEFAULT 0,
  -- Behavioral/meta signals (populated by task_206 service)
  exif_freshness REAL DEFAULT 0,
  capture_context REAL DEFAULT 0,
  session_context REAL DEFAULT 0,
  file_metadata REAL DEFAULT 0,
  duplicate_hash REAL DEFAULT 0,
  trust_tier_bonus REAL DEFAULT 0,
  -- Aggregates
  total_score REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  max_possible INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 12,
  verdict TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'accepted',
  dhash TEXT,
  processing_ms INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_photo_verif_user ON photo_verifications(user_id);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_photo_verif_verdict ON photo_verifications(verdict);
```

Add migration check in `database.ts` `initializeDatabase()` using the existing pattern:
```typescript
if (!(await this.isMigrationApplied('024_photo_verification'))) {
  await pool.query(`...SQL above...`);
  await this.recordMigration('024_photo_verification');
}
```

#### 2. New service: `backend/node-services/api-server/src/services/photo-cv-analysis.ts`

Export a single entry point + 6 internal analyzers. All analysis runs on a 200x200 downscaled grayscale/RGB buffer for speed (<100ms total target).

```typescript
export interface CVSignalResult {
  name: string;
  score: number;      // 0.0 - 1.0
  passed: boolean;    // score >= threshold
  details: string;    // human-readable
}

export interface CVAnalysisResult {
  signals: CVSignalResult[];
  passedCount: number;
  processingMs: number;
}

export async function analyzeCVSignals(imageBuffer: Buffer): Promise<CVAnalysisResult>
```

**6 signal implementations (all use sharp, already installed):**

1. **`analyzeColorDiversity(raw, width, height)`** — Convert RGB pixels to HSL, build hue histogram (12 bins of 30°), compute Shannon entropy. Food: entropy > 2.0 → pass. Threshold: 0.4.

2. **`analyzeSaturationWarmth(raw, width, height)`** — Compute % of pixels with hue in warm range (0-60° and 300-360° on HSL wheel) AND saturation > 0.3. Food: >35% warm → pass. Threshold: 0.35.

3. **`analyzeTextureVariance(raw, width, height)`** — Convert to grayscale, divide into 8×8 grid of patches, compute stddev of luminance per patch, take mean of all patches. Food: mean stddev > 12 → pass. Threshold: 0.3.

4. **`analyzeMultiRegion(raw, width, height)`** — Quantize to 8 colors (simple median-cut or nearest in a fixed palette), flood-fill to count distinct contiguous regions > 1% of image area. Food: 4+ regions → pass. Threshold: 0.3.

5. **`analyzeSpecularHighlights(raw, width, height)`** — Find pixels with luminance > 240 that have neighbors with luminance < 200 (isolated bright spots = oil/sauce shine). Count clusters. Food: 2+ clusters → pass. Threshold: 0.2.

6. **`analyzeCircularContours(raw, width, height)`** — Apply 3×3 Sobel edge kernels (horizontal + vertical) via manual convolution on grayscale buffer. In the center 60% of the image, measure edge density and check for curved edge patterns (adjacent edge pixels that change direction gradually). Plates/bowls create arcs. Edge density > 0.15 in center + curvature detected → pass. Threshold: 0.3.

**Implementation approach:**
```typescript
import sharp from 'sharp';

const ANALYSIS_SIZE = 200; // downscale target

export async function analyzeCVSignals(imageBuffer: Buffer): Promise<CVAnalysisResult> {
  const start = Date.now();
  const { data: raw, info } = await sharp(imageBuffer)
    .resize(ANALYSIS_SIZE, ANALYSIS_SIZE, { fit: 'fill' })
    .removeAlpha()
    .raw()
    .toBuffer({ resolveWithObject: true });

  const width = info.width;
  const height = info.height;

  // Run all 6 analyzers
  const signals: CVSignalResult[] = [
    analyzeColorDiversity(raw, width, height),
    analyzeSaturationWarmth(raw, width, height),
    analyzeTextureVariance(raw, width, height),
    analyzeMultiRegion(raw, width, height),
    analyzeSpecularHighlights(raw, width, height),
    analyzeCircularContours(raw, width, height),
  ];

  return {
    signals,
    passedCount: signals.filter(s => s.passed).length,
    processingMs: Date.now() - start,
  };
}
```

Each analyzer is a pure function taking `(raw: Buffer, width: number, height: number)` and returning `CVSignalResult`. No async needed since we already have the raw buffer.

### Subtasks
1. Add migration 024 SQL to `database.ts` `initializeDatabase()`
2. Create `photo-cv-analysis.ts` with the 6 signal analyzers
3. Add helper: `rgbToHsl(r, g, b)` for color space conversion
4. Add helper: `sobelEdgeDetect(gray, width, height)` for edge detection
5. Export `analyzeCVSignals()` entry point
6. Test with `curl` by temporarily adding a test endpoint (remove before PR)

### Depends on
none

### Acceptance criteria
- Given a food photo buffer, when `analyzeCVSignals()` is called, then it returns 6 signal results with scores 0-1 in <200ms
- Given a screenshot/solid-color image, when analyzed, then most CV signals score below their thresholds
- Given the migration runs, then `photo_verifications` table exists with all columns and indexes
- PostgreSQL `$N` placeholders used everywhere (never `?`)

### Files to create/modify
- `backend/node-services/api-server/src/services/photo-cv-analysis.ts` (NEW)
- `backend/node-services/api-server/src/services/database.ts` (add migration 024)
